rationale.R1.2: The section constructs a load-bearing logical chain (Harmony benchmark rank → MethodSelector default → central contribution); that argument must be internally consistent regardless of which edit type the user intends.
rationale.R1.3: The entire architectural justification rests on a specific empirical claim attributed to Luecken et al. (2022): that Harmony is the top performer on the bio-conservation axis of scIB. Whether that characterization accurately reflects the paper's findings is non-trivially at stake.
rationale.R1.6: The phrasing 'help me with this section' most commonly signals a writing-polish request; the artifact shows verbatim or near-verbatim repetition of the Harmony ranking claim across all three paragraphs, a clear structural prose problem.
rationale.R1.7: The section's entire evidential authority derives from a single citation; correct characterization of that source's findings — and whether the superlative framing ('clearly dominates', 'top-ranked', 'leading performer') is warranted — is load-bearing if the task is scientific rather than stylistic.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want writing-quality improvements to this section (prose clarity, flow, eliminating the repetitive restatement of the Harmony claim), or do you want the scientific claims verified for accuracy — specifically, whether Luecken et al. (2022) actually rank Harmony first on the biological-conservation axis of the scIB benchmark?
